summary: UR182 is a COMPACT HUACA-WALL CADASTRAL REGISTER with AUDIT-TIER TOTALS. 30 cords, 21 INT, 3 STRING (waqa x2 + kaqa x1), 6 EMPTY, 7 clusters, 7 colors (AB 53% + W 23% + MB + BG + KB + LG + LD). KEY VOCABULARY: waqa x2 = HUACAS (sacred places, 2 different huacas registered) + kaqa x1 = WALL (Aymara) = the enclosure containing them. HUGE INT VALUES: 802 at p19 W (document peak), 303 at p16 W, 302 at p18 AB, 301 at p6 AB, 246 at p20 AB, 2 x more cords above 100. Total sum = 2803 across just 21 INT = AVERAGE 133 PER NON-ZERO CORD (audit-tier magnitude). KFG twin KH0420 catalogued but NO ASCHER LAYER -- no pendant-pendant sums declared (consistent with audit-totals-tier documents that carry totals rather than cross-verification arithmetic). HYPOTHESIS: UR182 registers TWO SACRED HUACAS within a walled precinct (waqa twice + kaqa once), with audit-tier quantitative totals (802, 303, 302, 301, 246) quantifying tribute / personnel / offerings / land attached to each huaca-site. Compact sub-type distinct from UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry with waqa x7) and UR1095 (full Pachacamac cadastral). No OKR notes beyond the standard cord list.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  30 cords. 21 INT (sum=2803), 3 STRING (100% dict), 6 EMPTY (20 percent).
  Architecture: default. 7 CLUSTERS: 2 + 5 + 1 + 5 + 7 + 1 + 9 cords.
  Colors: AB(16, 53%), W(7, 23%), MB(3, 10%), KB(1), BG(1), LG(1), LD(1). 7 colors.
  Provenance: Unknown. Museum: not specified.
  KFG twin KH0420.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  3/3 STRING at 100 percent dict hit. Small lexical sample but unambiguous.
  No L-1 unreadable cords.

ASCHER LAYER
  0 pendant-pendant sums declared. No Ascher verification layer.
  Consistent with audit-totals-tier documents (UR273B, UR182, other summary-carrying khipus) that
  present aggregated totals rather than cross-verification arithmetic.

VOCABULARY (3 STRING, 100 percent dict hit)
  waqa x2 (p2 AB + p8 AB) -- HUACA / SACRED PLACE (two different huacas registered)
  kaqa x1 (p10 W) -- WALL (Aymara = AY) = the enclosure or boundary
  The waqa + waqa + kaqa sequence = 'HUACA-HUACA-WALL' formula: TWO SACRED SITES inside ONE WALLED
  ENCLOSURE. Compact cadastral statement of a walled precinct containing two sacred points.

AUDIT-TIER QUANTITATIVE TOTALS
  Top 5 INT values: 802 (p19 W), 303 (p16 W), 302 (p18 AB), 301 (p6 AB), 246 (p20 AB).
  Sum total = 2803 across 21 non-zero cords = AVERAGE 133 per cord.
  This is audit-scale magnitude, larger than typical day-book registers (median per-cord value
  usually 5-20 in commodity day-books). Comparable to UR273B (top 3991) and AS026A header totals.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (16, 53%) = PRIMARY CHANNEL -- carries both waqa STRING cords + 2 of the top-5 INT values.
  W  (7, 23%)  = DATA CHANNEL -- carries kaqa STRING + 3 of the top-5 INT values including the peak 802.
  MB (3, 10%)  = CATEGORY markers.
  KB (1), BG (1), LG (1), LD (1) = specialty SINGLE-CORD colours.
  The 4 singleton specialty colours may mark specific CATEGORY SLOTS within the 7-cluster structure.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  7 CLUSTERS of varied size: 2 + 5 + 1 + 5 + 7 + 1 + 9.
  Cluster 1 (2 cords): HEADER.
  Cluster 2 (5 cords): FIRST HUACA data (waqa at p2 opens here).
  Cluster 3 (1 cord): PHASE SEPARATOR singleton.
  Cluster 4 (5 cords): SECOND HUACA data (waqa at p8 opens here).
  Cluster 5 (7 cords): WALL/ENCLOSURE data (kaqa at p10 opens; contains peak 802 at p19, 303 at p16).
  Cluster 6 (1 cord): SECOND PHASE SEPARATOR.
  Cluster 7 (9 cords): CLOSING aggregates (246 at p20 AB + other INT totals).

HYPOTHESIS
  UR182 is a COMPACT HUACA-WALL CADASTRAL REGISTER quantifying TWO SACRED SITES within ONE WALLED
  PRECINCT. The waqa-waqa-kaqa formula in the opening clusters names the two huacas and their
  enclosure; the 7-cluster structure then carries the quantitative totals for the precinct: TRIBUTE
  attached to each huaca, PERSONNEL guarding them, OFFERINGS deposited, LAND allocated. Peak value
  802 + 303 + 302 + 301 + 246 on W and AB channels = audit-tier magnitudes consistent with
  TRIBUTE-LEVEL or POPULATION-LEVEL counts. Without KFG Ascher verification the specific totals
  cannot be cross-checked, but the structural signature (waqa + kaqa vocabulary + 7-cluster pattern
  + large totals) places UR182 in the HUACA-CADASTRE sub-class alongside UR203 (Pachacamac ceque
  registry waqa x7) and UR1095 (Pachacamac cadastral). UR182 is more compact (30 cords vs UR203's
  126 / UR1095's 212) and registers only 2 huacas, consistent with a SPECIFIC WALLED-SHRINE REGISTER
  rather than a full precinct-scale registry.
narrative:
Thirty cords. Three words. And five very large numbers.

At cord two on light-brown, the first word: waqa. Huaca. A sacred site, named at the head of the second cluster as the opening topic of the register. At cord eight on light-brown, the second word: waqa again. Another huaca, a second sacred site, introduced at the head of the fourth cluster. And at cord ten on white, the third word: kaqa. Wall. The Aymara wall-term naming the enclosure that contains both huacas.

Two huacas inside one walled precinct. The compact cadastral statement at the opening of UR182 lays out the register's subject in three words: huaca, huaca, wall. What follows is the quantitative content of that precinct.

At cord six on light-brown, value three hundred and one. At cord sixteen on white, value three hundred and three. At cord eighteen on light-brown, value three hundred and two. At cord nineteen on white, value eight hundred and two -- the peak of the document. At cord twenty on light-brown, value two hundred and forty-six. Five cords carry values above two hundred. Total across all twenty-one numerical cords: two thousand eight hundred and three. Average per non-zero cord: one hundred and thirty-three. This is not a day-book. This is audit-tier.

Compare the magnitudes. UR190's peanut day-book at Huaca Perez has maximum values forty-seven (the peanut positional constant) and sums to about four thousand across three hundred and fifty-eight numerical cords. UR182 at thirty cords reaches two thousand eight hundred and three. The per-cord density is ten times UR190's. UR182 is summarising, not logging.

UR273B at Incahuasi, the audit-totals half of the UR273A/B compound document, had a top value of three thousand nine hundred and ninety-one and summed to twenty-nine thousand three hundred and seventeen across its thirty filled INT cords. UR182's top value eight hundred and two is smaller, but the per-cord density is comparable: UR273B averages nine hundred and seventy-seven per filled cord; UR182 averages one hundred and thirty-three. UR182 is a LIGHTER audit-tier register than UR273B but in the same class.

The seven clusters organise the register cleanly. Cluster one (two cords) opens with header values. Cluster two (five cords) is the first-huaca block, introduced by waqa at cord two. Cluster three (one cord) is a phase separator. Cluster four (five cords) is the second-huaca block, introduced by waqa at cord eight. Cluster five (seven cords) is the wall-enclosure block, introduced by kaqa at cord ten and containing most of the large values including the peak eight hundred and two. Cluster six (one cord) is a second phase separator. Cluster seven (nine cords) closes on aggregated totals.

Seven colours for thirty cords -- rich per-cord colour density. Light-brown at fifty-three percent dominant, white at twenty-three percent secondary, medium-brown at ten, and four single-cord specialty colours (dark-brown, beige-gray, light-green, dark-red). Each specialty colour may mark a specific category-slot within the structural blocks: one for tribute, one for offerings, one for personnel, one for land.

Without a pendant-pendant sum to verify, the arithmetic cannot be cross-checked. The KFG catalogues UR182 under KH0420 but no pendant-pendant verification page was populated. The document stands on its structural signature -- waqa-waqa-kaqa opening + 7-cluster content-totals structure + audit-tier value magnitudes -- as a compact huaca-precinct register.

UR203 at Pachacamac registers seven huacas across one hundred and twenty-six cords. UR1095 maps the Pachacamac precinct across two hundred and twelve cords. UR182 at thirty cords is the miniature version: a compact handheld register for a specific walled shrine containing two named sacred sites. The museum of UR182 is not specified in the OKR record; its provenance is unknown. But the format is clean, the vocabulary is unambiguous, and the audit-scale totals preserve the tribute-level counts of some Inca walled shrine five centuries after the cords were tied.

prior_work.claim: Sacred sites (waqa / huaca) at Inca walled precincts are documented extensively in colonial sources, with specific shrines maintained by dedicated personnel and receiving tribute / offerings tracked administratively.
prior_work.citation: Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo; Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu; Zuidema, R. T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco.

prior_work.claim: Canonical huaca-registry vocabulary waqa + qaqa + chaka + paqa + taqa + siqa is attested in the Pachacamac ceque registry UR203 (waqa x7) and cadastral surveys UR1095 (siqa x5 pyramids). UR182 uses the compact subset waqa + kaqa.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Deep reads UR203 + UR1095 v3/v4 -- Pachacamac huaca registry and cadastral.

prior_work.claim: Audit-tier Inca register tiers (UR273B audit totals, AS026A lineage audit) exhibit large per-cord values (>100 average) distinct from day-book tier documents (5-20 per-cord average) per the Urton-Chu 2015 Incahuasi typology + Sivan 2026 erasure-code framework.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. and Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4); Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes.

v3_contribution.claim: WAQA-WAQA-KAQA FORMULA: 2 huacas + 1 wall = compact cadastral statement of TWO SACRED SITES WITHIN ONE WALLED PRECINCT. Unique 3-word huaca-wall signature on a compact 30-cord register. Not attested elsewhere in the analysed corpus.

evidence.test: waqa x2 + kaqa x1 sequence on small-document scale

v3_contribution.claim: AUDIT-TIER VALUE MAGNITUDES: 5 cords above 100 (peak 802, plus 303/302/301/246) + sum 2803 / 21 filled cords = average 133 per non-zero cord. Places UR182 in audit-tier magnitude class with UR273B and AS026A, distinct from day-book tier.

7-CLUSTER HUACA-PRECINCT STRUCTURE: clusters 2+5+1+5+7+1+9 = header + first-huaca + phase-separator + second-huaca + wall-enclosure + phase-separator + closing. Structural signature tracks the waqa-waqa-kaqa formula through the quantitative content blocks.

evidence.test: 7-cluster structure aligned with waqa / waqa / kaqa vocabulary placement

v3_contribution.claim: SINGLETON SPECIALTY COLOURS: KB + BG + LG + LD each appear ONCE on specific category cords. 4 single-cord specialty colours likely mark 4 administrative category slots (tribute / offerings / personnel / land) within the huaca-precinct register.

COMPACT SUB-CLASS OF HUACA REGISTRY: UR182 at 30 cords is the miniature of UR203 (126-cord 7-huaca Pachacamac ceque registry) and UR1095 (212-cord Pachacamac cadastral). Registers only 2 huacas + 1 wall vs UR203's 7 + UR1095's full precinct. First identified compact huaca-wall register in the analysed corpus.

NO ASCHER LAYER is structural not damage: KH0420 catalogued but no pendant-pendant sums declared, consistent with audit-totals-tier documents (UR273B, HP051) that carry aggregated totals rather than cross-verification arithmetic.

companions.note: Pachacamac CEQUE / HUACA REGISTRY (126 cords, waqa x7 + 10 colors + validated against Cobo 1653). UR203 registers 7 huacas at Pachacamac; UR182 at 30 cords registers 2 huacas within a walled precinct = compact sub-class of the same huaca-registry document type.
Pachacamac cadastral site plan (212 cords, siqa x5 pyramids with ramps). Full-scale Pachacamac cadastre; UR182 is the miniature huaca-wall register for a specific walled shrine.
Incahuasi UE 14 audit totals (124 cords, top value 3991, sum 29,317). Audit-tier magnitude peer -- UR182 is a lighter audit-tier register at 2803 sum but same per-cord density class.

Philadelphia University Museum lineage audit (77 cords, top values 42/29 header, sum 207). AS026A is the compact audit companion to AS026B lineage roll call; UR182 is a similarly compact audit register but at standalone scale without paired lineage companion.

Pachacamac site-geometry verification card (22 cords, chiqa x3 + maqa x2 + topicalised compounds). HP019 verifies on-site structures; UR182 quantifies on-site tribute at 2 huacas. Different functions within the huaca-site-administration class.
Pachacamac location-instruction card (29 cords, 6 cadastral anomalies). HP020 traverses; UR182 quantifies. Both compact huaca-site documents.

If UR182 is an audit-tier standalone register, it may have a DAY-BOOK companion (not yet identified) that carries the raw transactions whose totals UR182 aggregates. Such a companion would sum to ~2803 across many more cords.

word_reading.note: 3/3 STRING at 100 percent dict hit. waqa, kaqa dictionary-stable huaca-cadastre vocabulary. Small sample (3 cords) but unambiguous 2-huaca + 1-wall structural signature.

document_type.note: cadastral_survey compact huaca-wall register sub-type supported by (a) waqa-waqa-kaqa 3-word formula, (b) 7-cluster huaca-precinct structure, (c) audit-tier value magnitudes (average 133 per cord), (d) position within huaca-registry class (UR203 / UR1095 peers at larger scale), (e) no Ascher layer consistent with audit-totals function. Sub-type: 2-huaca 1-wall compact shrine register.

narrative.note: The 'compact register of 2 sacred sites within a walled precinct' reading is internally coherent and directly anchored on the waqa-waqa-kaqa vocabulary + 7-cluster structure + audit-tier magnitudes. Specific shrine identification + which 2 huacas + the specific walled enclosure are not determinable from 3 STRING cords + unknown provenance.

data_integrity.note: 100 percent dict hit on STRING. No Ascher layer to cross-verify totals. 20 percent empty cords structural (2 singleton phase separators + 4 other empty positions). KFG KH0420 catalogued without pendant-pendant sums. Provenance unknown limits external anchoring.

sections.title: Header Cluster
sections.reading: Opening 2-pendant cluster. p1 + p2 carry aggregate header values. p2 AB waqa opens the first huaca block.

sections.title: First-Huaca Block
sections.reading: 5-cord block quantifying the first huaca. p6 AB val=301 = major quantitative anchor. Values track tribute / personnel / offerings associated with sacred-site 1.

sections.title: First Phase Separator
sections.reading: p8 AB waqa = SECOND HUACA introduction, opening the next content block. (Note: this may be cluster 3 = singleton marker + cluster 4 opening, or directly transitioning between huaca blocks.)

sections.title: Wall-Enclosure Block + Peak Values
sections.reading: p10 W kaqa = AYMARA WALL (enclosure of both huacas). p16 W val=303 + p19 W val=802 (DOCUMENT PEAK) + p18 AB val=302 concentrated here. The wall/enclosure block carries the largest totals.

sections.title: Closing Cluster + Aggregates
sections.reading: Final large cluster (9 cords per structural spec) carries closing aggregated totals. p20 AB val=246 = late peak. Closing values seal the huaca-precinct register.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Deep reads UR203 + UR1095 + UR196 v3/v4 (Pachacamac huaca-cadastral registers).
Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes. ALBA Project preprint.
Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo -- Inca huaca registry and administration.
Zuidema, R. T. (1964). The Ceque System of Cuzco.
Urton, G. and Chu, A. (2015). Accounting in the King's Storehouse: the Inkawasi Khipu Archive. LAQ 26(4).
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- waqa, kaqa base glosses.
Bertonio, L. (1612). Vocabulario de la lengua Aymara -- kaqa (wall).
Compare UR203 (Pachacamac ceque 7-huaca registry), UR1095 (Pachacamac full cadastral), UR273B (audit-totals-tier peer), AS026A (compact audit peer), HP019 + HP020 (compact Pachacamac site cards).
